mentions.note: One of Kramer's earliest and most successful long-term picks; early recognition of AI potential and chip leadership. Multiple viewers credited Nvidia as portfolio cornerstone generating seven-figure returns.

Best chips in world with software moat vs. AMD, Google, others. Up 39% in 2025. Jensen Wong's CES keynote showed Blackwell in full production, H2 2026 shipments ahead of schedule; physical AI (robots, autonomous vehicles) massive. Trading <25x forward earnings vs. 40% growth—insanely cheap. Own it, don't trade it.
Consistently recommended for club members; no revelation given prior recommendations; at elevated levels.
Weak stock despite steady drumbeat of good news reflects portfolio rebalancing, not deteriorating fundamentals. Own it, don't trade it.
Opened AI-drug development facility with Eli Lilly—transformative breakthrough but market treats as sideshow; stock stagnant despite industry momentum.
Secular growth stock exemplar—own don't trade. Cramer met millionaires at JPMorgan conference who held through volatility; example of buy-and-hold philosophy for secular growers, not traders.
Taiwan Semi's biggest customer; stock up 4 points validating demand thesis; higher-quality entry point as bubble skepticism fades after TSM confirmation.
Great company but trading pattern erratic; likely to close down despite ups during day; greatest technologist Jensen Wong correctly forecasted AI/data-center boom when others missed it.
Only company that truly foresaw AI/data-center demand scaling; partnered with TSMC for high-end chips; no bottleneck, no shortage; Jensen Wong greatest technologist of era; will continue to lead.
Semiconductor leader; only company to foresee data center boom scale; strategic tech holding; closed down despite being up in day.
Mega-cap tech pillar down 4% today, 15%+ from Oct highs; high-quality name being sold to fund speculative plays; no longer a best buy in frothy market.
Highest-quality chip stock down 4% today, off 15% from late October high; money flowing out of quality mega-cap tech into speculative names—dangerous rotation.
AI data center buildout lynchpin; demand from OpenAI and hyperscalers remains strong but exposed if OpenAI fundraising or market share deteriorates.

Part of Magnificent 7 facing P/E multiple compression (from 30x to 22x) as money rotates to storage plays; Cramer owns in travel trust but expects eventual reflow of capital back to mega-cap tech leaders.
Rallied on rumor Chinese government allowing H200 chip sales to ByteDance, Tencent, Alibaba. Story's veracity uncertain.

$2B CoreWeave investment validates demand ('incredible' per Jensen). Bears unconvinced despite clear fundamentals. Own it, don't trade it; sellers will be wrong long-term. Allocation scarcity, not demand, is the issue CoreWeave helps solve.

Invested $2B in CoreWeave at $87.20/share, validating demand and preferred-partner status. Jensen Huang: demand incredible, applications through roof. CoreWeave deal proves ecosystem health, not vendor financing. Despite daily battleground noise, sellers repeatedly proven wrong. Own don't trade — patience pays.

Up only 1% YTD despite strength; should catch up with Mag 7 peers; CEO Jensen Wong in China during lunar new year exploring meaningful Chinese business; 25% of China sales going to U.S. government per deal.
Runs the game, not playing it; undisputed AI chip leader; power infrastructure tailwind ensures dominance.
CEO Jensen Wong only one who saw chip shortage coming and stocked accordingly; visionary leadership on AI and data-center capacity.
Still magnificent despite terrible stock action; coiled spring at 24x earnings. Up 55% YTD. Jensen Wong interview tomorrow to clear up OpenAI friction stories. Buy on weakness.
Clobbered on ill-advised OpenAI friction stories; 24x earnings trades as coiled spring; up 55% YoY; Jensen Huang interview tomorrow to clear confusion.
Hardware winner in AI era; benefits from enterprise software spending and infrastructure buildout; will prevail if backed by great company fundamentals.
Strategic 25-year partnership with Dassault on physical AI, digital twins across aerospace, autos, pharma, robots; integrating CUDA/Omniverse; massive $90T real-economy opportunity.
Hasn't reported yet; all Mag 7 capex forecasts validate Nvidia demand; soared 8% today (best since April 2025); beneficiary of data center spending; own, don't trade.
Hasn't reported yet; getting big cut of Mag 7 capex; soared 8% today on speculation; best session since April 2024; own don't trade; many had given up on it.

Spectacular earnings quarter with amazing growth, fantastic margins, new clients (Anthropic, OpenAI), and terrific guidance; own it don't trade it. Today's $10 decline was purely program-driven selling unrelated to fundamentals.
One of two conviction stocks with own-it-don't-trade-it mentality; down heavily Feb 27 but Cramer standing pat; painful but philosophy has worked; own it don't trade it.
New inference chip announcement re-energizes market; company largest on Earth and psychologically critical to broader sentiment. Rally +3% on news.
Preferred over Broadcom/Poor; positive work in last 72 hours; Cramer attention returning.
Climbed ~$3 on pure sentiment shift; AI buildout tailwind intact. No fundamental news but buyers returning to highest-risk names after week of fear-driven selling.
White House rumors of export restrictions on AI accelerators threaten growth; could hand market to China if implemented.
Core holding for long-time investor (Chris) from pandemic low; strong performer; maintain position.
Semiconductor surge on oil reversal; buy-buy-buy. No new catalyst, just relief expectation data center demand resumes.
Invested $2B in Coherent, cementing strategic partnership; customer 20+ years; seeking security of supply for advanced optical technologies; validates data center AI infrastructure secular growth.
Announced $2B investment in Nebius AI cloud company; GTC conference next week will showcase continued data center momentum. Theme can shine once war ends.
Cramer was early to recognize AI potential and Nvidia's leadership position. One viewer credits Nvidia as their largest portfolio holding, worth over $1M at 30% of their wealth.
Tripled demand forecast to $1 trillion by end 2027; new inference chips address customer needs; ecosystem kingpin with unmatched profitability still at 183—stock undervalued.

Stock down today despite 'incredible numbers' from GTC keynote; don't panic. Held back by option sellers and retail trading; own it, don't trade it. Fundamentals are 'the real deal' and will ultimately drive higher prices.

Vera Rubin CPU, Open Claw, digital biology (Eli Lilly), AI agents, robotics not yet in guidance or priced in; 21 months to end-2027 to book new businesses beyond $1T guidance. Possible first $10-20 trillion company. Stock at $183 will mean nothing in retrospect.
Fastest-growing large-cap at lowest valuation; Nemo Claw agents driving next hyperscaler capex wave. Own it, don't trade it.
Caller John bullish that stock will double; Cramer endorses thesis implicitly via Dell server vendor-financing advantage; Nvidia chips now principal way businesses buy servers via Dell.
World's largest company now trades cheaper than Sherwin-Williams (paint co.) on P/E—absurd valuation. But could get even cheaper Monday. Contrarian investing works both ways; wait for real catalyst.
King of AI hardware; critical compute supplier to OpenAI, Anthropic, and all AI upstarts; vertical wall of agentic demand requires massive chip inventory.
War has minor impact; strong AI demand and data center capex continue. Check oil/rate headwinds & demand durability first; watch for Iran deal catalyst. Better long-term hold than near-term trade.
Own it don't trade it; stock falling despite strong GTC showing and great fundamentals due to broad tech selloff; will recover but timing unknown—don't chase lower.

Down 20% from October highs, trading 15x forward earnings (vs. historical 18-20x). War/oil shock created valuation compression; stock acts as multiple-shrinking proxy. Risk: multiples could compress further (14x, 13x) if war persists. Hold but don't chase; wait for war resolution or PE stabilization.
Trading at 14x earnings—absurdly cheap valuation for AI leader with unmatched chip dominance; war premium unwind will re-rate multiples higher.
Started strong on Day 2 but fizzled; deserves premium multiple but hasn't done much lately; Cramer wants to see it lead rally.
Down a great deal, suddenly disliked—exactly when you want to own it; prefer over Taiwan Semi.
Hardware dominance redefined; AI chip ecosystem replicates Microsoft/Intel 1980s–90s takeover; customers wish they could buy more compute.
Johnny's portfolio: finally breaking out; core tech holding in well-diversified portfolio with retail, copper, banking; own it, don't trade it.
Finally breaking out; critical to multiple portfolios; growth story remains intact despite competitive pressures and past enthusiasm.
Own it, don't trade it. AI leadership and optical infrastructure positioning insulate from geopolitical noise.
Bounced from 165 to 196 after relentless sell-off; worst fears (China ban, Google chips, circular deals) never materialized; forward PE just 17x; now back in favor alongside Mag 7 resurgence.
Preferred way to gain GPU/compute exposure vs. speculative shoe-to-AI pivots; Jensen Huang knows the industry; real operator.
Biggest laggard in AI rally despite being the inventor; trades 15x earnings vs. S&P 500 average; controls memory, compute, speed; Anthropic best client; buy on any dip ahead of May 20 earnings.
Portfolio recommendation for college fund; greatest tech stock; must-own for long-term growth in AI era.
CEO Jensen Huang articulates five-layer AI cake; GPU shortages drive relentless demand. Money managers can't get enough; stock won't sustain declines.
Caller questions valuation amid high spending and competition; Cramer declines to speculate on near-term moves.
GPU dominance in data center secular AI buildout; mentioned as part of broad data center complex driving market.
Unstoppable momentum; zero sellers at $216, up $8 on Friday too. Own it, don't trade it. Heart of fourth industrial revolution.
Owned for trust; weather macro volatility via Fourth Industrial Revolution capex (compute, storage, power) thesis.
Not one of the big dogs reporting tonight; market needs shortage story, not just growth. AI everywhere with no scarcity.
Jensen Huang's 'fourth industrial revolution' thesis proving out; white-collar job displacement accelerating this earnings season.
Deep partnership with Amazon; AWS best place to run Nvidia; customers want choice; competition healthy for ecosystem.
Started AI revolution and remains largest company on earth; stock being left behind relative to broader infrastructure gains but still core beneficiary.
Core AI hardware infrastructure play driving data center buildout; renamed Cramer's dog after it; fundamental to AI revolution.

Jensen Huang leading largest infrastructure buildout in human history. Nvidia's versatile architecture runs every AI model; all major players (OpenAI, Anthropic, Meta, Gemini, XAI) adopting platform. Partnership with Corning expanding supply chain.
Founder Jensen Huang leading fourth industrial revolution (AI/accelerated computing). Core thesis intact; P/E multiples justified by infrastructure buildout scale and supply chain dominance.
Foundational data center stock; must-own for AI buildout; buy on dips or pay up rather than miss it; don't make it entire portfolio.
Referenced as AI infrastructure leader; part of data center/GPU build-out; fundamental to hyperscaler compute.
Notably slow starter in yesterday's data center rally; unlike other memory/semis, Nvidia didn't lead early move; Cramer approves of data center group correction as healthy; no specific downside call.

Jensen Huang's visionary leadership and AI chip dominance earned 'own it, don't trade it' status; September 2022 interview at $120 bottoms preceded massive AI boom; credible management earned benefit of doubt.

Trading cheaper than S&P 500 average on forward earnings despite leading generative AI race. Own it; far cheaper than peers (AMD 21x, Broadcom 33x, Cerebras 187x sales) despite superior growth and profitability.
Most important stock in entire market; heart of data center buildout; own it, don't trade it. Growth should be spectacular; might get perfect quarter needed for much higher move.

Magnificent Seven member owned by Cramer's charitable trust. AI chip supplier with secular megatrend tailwinds; dominant market position; ability to scale into enormous enterprise; recession-resistant.

Pounded the table since $4 split-adjusted; Q1 earnings exceptional (85% revenue growth, $81.6B beat by $3B, free cash flow beat $10B); dominant position in AI chip market; capital redeployment challenge signals maturity; stock saturation is function of size, not quality.

Leading AI chip maker with fantastic long-term story; makes most powerful semiconductors for cutting-edge applications. Insanely volatile stock ideal for trading around core position—Cramer's stance: own it, don't trade it.
Cramer's best pick ever; always looked expensive on trailing P/E but had exceptional forward earnings growth. Own it, don't trade it — recommended for long-term portfolios.

Reported strong quarter but stock down; only +15% YTD vs. booming semiconductor peers. Needs pivot toward Apple-style buybacks and dividend strategy; best-in-class AI tech no longer sufficient to drive stock appreciation.
Fundamentally still liked and not backing away, but $3 drop on no news is inexplicable; waiting for Computex presentation this weekend to clarify and see if Jensen unveils something special.

Computex keynote delivered; Jensen revealed RTX Spark Superchip (ARM-based), aggressive pricing, and new product roadmap; truly delivered when least expected; dominance reasserts; Nvidia least expensive in group.
Jensen Wong's endorsements of data center customers irresistible; more chip spending = more profits. Own it, don't trade it.
Core AI story and Jensen Huang's leadership remain excellent, but expect significant selling pressure as investors liquidate this mega-cap to fund IPO participation; near-term volatility expected.
Stock playing shoots-and-ladders with shareholders; Goldman Sachs mojo absent; lots of unknown risks despite chip dominance; no clear catalyst.

Linchpin of entire market; powers generative AI, agents, training. Sovereign AI programs (14% of revenue, accelerating) emerging as powerful customer base. Google paying SpaceX $920M/month for Nvidia chips (vs. Google TPUs) proves market confidence. Own don't trade despite mega-IPO headwinds.
Only mega-cap tech with bulletproof balance sheet and robust buyback; will snap back first from oversupply selloff. Best positioned to benefit from AI infrastructure buildout.
Own it, don't trade it; same liquidation story as Apple. Most IPO capital will end up buying Nvidia equipment anyway—don't panic on tactical weakness.
Cramer's favorite in semiconductors; done nothing lately but fundamentals strong; prefer over AMD but both terrific at these levels.
While a powerful technology leader, Cramer warns against concentrating retirement savings in single stocks; prefer diversified index funds for long-term buy-and-hold investing.
Hyperscaler raising $25B in debt despite fortress balance sheet; benefiting from massive AI capex cycle and hyperscaler competition driving continued chip demand.
Jensen Wong saw data center demand coming in memory space; hyperscaler demand driving chip shortage; Cramer assumes Nvidia's dominance continues.
Named as great momentum stock alongside Eli Lilly; exemplifies need for longer-term price target methodology on high-growth names.
Exemplifies high-growth companies that consistently beat forward-earnings estimates, making the stock appear cheap in hindsight; secret to sustained outperformance since 2012.
